findings.evidence: The HTML rendering displays a seemingly styled version of the page content at the top, but then appends a completely unformatted plain-text dump of the exact same content at the bottom of the page. A reader would read the entire document twice per page.
Similar to page 1, the HTML appends a raw, unformatted text dump of the entire page content below the styled HTML version.

In the duplicated raw text section at the bottom of the HTML page, the alphabetical list under item 5 (A through G) loses its line breaks and list structure, rendering as a single run-on block of text.
The HTML rendering appends a raw, unformatted text dump of the entire page content below the styled HTML version.
The HTML rendering appends a raw, unformatted text dump of the entire page content below the styled HTML version, which also drops the visual signature image present in the top portion.
